ground_truth.title: Affordable Software Development Degree | BS in Computer Science - The University of Texas Permian Basin | UTPB
ground_truth.main_content: Software Development Degree Track - Bachelor of Science (BS) in Computer Science with Software Development Track. Power The Future Through Code. The Software Development Track prepares students for one of the most in-demand fields in today's tech-driven world. This track emphasizes hands-on experience in software engineering, database design, web and multimedia technologies, and mobile app development—equipping graduates with the skills to create innovative, real-world solutions across platforms. Degree Requirements: 120 total credit hours required for graduation, 48 upper-level credit hours and a minor are required, All required and elective courses must be completed with a grade of C- or better, Computer Science Core (25 hours), Software Development Track (19 hours), and Mathematics (14 hours), Capstone Course, Freshman Seminar, and 16 hours of General Electives. Careers for Computer Science Majors with a Software Development Track: Software Developer, Computer Systems Analysts, Computer Support Specialists, Computer Network Architects, Computer Hardware Engineers, Database Administrators, Information Security Analysts. Key courses include: COSC 4415: Database Systems, COSC 4455: Multimedia & Web Development, COSC 4460: Software Engineering, COSC 4485: Mobile Application Development, COSC 4395: Research/Capstone project. Students complete 17 credit hours in math, including Calculus I & II, Intro to Probability, and Mathematical Reasoning. Students engage in practical, lab-based projects and complete the COSC 4395 capstone, emphasizing real-world software development and portfolio-building.
